mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as borrowing costs fall and liquidity expands.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows and forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a broad consumer demand contraction that reduces corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 55 percent over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.0 percent month-over-month during late-cycle expansions confirms robust output capacity that supports earnings momentum and generates positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative or flat S&P 500 returns in late-cycle periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5 for return differentials.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 2.5 percent year-over-year forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and triggers negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in over 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling GDP growth indicates labor market overheating without corresponding output expansion, prompting rate hike fears that suppress equity valuations.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-GDPC1 divergence signal shows a rank correlation below 0.01 with forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A negative real federal funds rate during disinflationary periods provides excessive monetary stimulus that fuels asset price inflation and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative real rates fail to generate positive forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) during disinflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1200 thousand units signals a severe construction sector downturn that drags on related industrial earnings and produces negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of instances where HOUST drops below the 1200 threshold.
